metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2332
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.4: "Overexpression of SALM2 Leads to Increases in the Number of Excitatory Synapses and Dendritic Spines"
Fig.5: "Mislocalized Expression of SALM2 Disrupts Excitatory Synapses and Dendritic Spines"
Fig.6: "Direct Aggregation of SALM2 Induces Clustering of Excitatory Postsynaptic Proteins"
Fig.7: "Knockdown of SALM2 by siRNA Leads to the Loss of Morphological and Functional Excitatory Synapses". Experimental description: Dissociated culture is of rat species: "Primary hippocampal cultures were prepared from embryonic day (E) 18-19 rat hippocampi described previously."

15/01/2018 Pim
- Overexpressed SALM2 is of rat species: ". Full-length rat SALM2 (aa 1-766) was PCR-amplified from a rat brain cDNA library"
